---
name: doc-banner
description: >-
  Generate an AI-rendered hero / flow banner image to place at the top of a
  markdown document (README, STORY, design doc). Use when the user asks for a
  "doc banner", "header image", "hero image for a doc", "flow banner", "banner
  for the top of the file", "title image", or wants to visualize a multi-step
  journey as a wide illustration. Renders via the Microsoft Foundry MAI image
  model (MAI-Image-2.5) and saves a PNG into the repo's images/ folder, then
  embeds it under the document H1.
---

# Doc Banner

Create a wide, partner-safe hero/flow banner for the top of a markdown file and
embed it under the H1. The image is generated by the **MAI-Image-2.5** model
deployed in Microsoft Foundry, not hand-drawn, so it is regenerable from a saved
prompt.

## When to use

- A document needs a visual "hero" at the top (README, `STORY.md`, a design doc).
- You want to show a **left → right multi-stage flow** as a polished illustration
  rather than ASCII art or a Mermaid diagram.
- You want the banner to be **reproducible** — the prompt lives in the doc/skill,
  and anyone can re-run the script to regenerate it.

## When NOT to use

- You need a **precise, editable** technical diagram (boxes that must match exact
  resource names, arrows that must be accurate) → use Mermaid or the `excalidraw`
  skill instead. Generated banners are decorative, not authoritative.
- The document is engagement output under `agent-output/<engagement>/` where the
  architecture agent already renders Mermaid → PNG. Keep those as-is.
- You cannot ground the visual in something real (don't invent product logos).

## Visual spec (house style)

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Aspect / size | Wide banner. **1344 × 768 px** (widest MAI supports — see constraints). |
| Composition | Horizontal left → right flow of numbered nodes connected by arrows. |
| Palette | Azure-aligned: deep blue `#0B3C8C` → teal/cyan `#2AA9B5` accents on a light/white background, subtle gradient. |
| Style | Clean **flat vector** tech illustration, modern, minimal, lots of whitespace. |
| Text | Stage names only (short labels). No paragraphs. Avoid long strings — keep words ≤ 12 chars. |
| Highlight | The stage owned by the current doc/repo is emphasized (brighter / larger node). |
| Safety | **No real or fake Microsoft/Azure logos, no trademarks, no real faces.** Generic geometric icons only. Partner-facing. |

### MAI image constraints (hard limits)

From [Deploy and use MAI image models in Microsoft Foundry](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/foundry/foundry-models/how-to/use-foundry-models-mai#api-endpoints):

- Output is **always PNG**.
- Each of `width` and `height` must be **≥ 768**.
- `width × height` must **not exceed 1,048,576** (= 1024 × 1024).
- Therefore the **widest usable banner is ~1344 × 768** (1,032,192 px, ≈ 16:9).
  A true 16:5 letterbox is **not** possible because height can't drop below 768.

## Prompt template (source of truth)

Keep this template in the doc (or here) so the banner is regenerable. Fill the
`{{...}}` placeholders, then pass the result to the script with `--prompt-file`.

```text
A clean, modern flat-vector hero banner illustration for a technical document.
Wide horizontal layout. A left-to-right process flow of {{N}} numbered circular
nodes connected by thin arrows, representing the stages: {{STAGE_1}}, {{STAGE_2}},
{{STAGE_3}}, {{STAGE_4}}, {{STAGE_5}}. Each node has a simple geometric icon and a
short label beneath it. Emphasize the "{{HIGHLIGHT_STAGE}}" node (larger, brighter).
Azure-aligned color palette: deep blue (#0B3C8C) transitioning to teal/cyan
(#2AA9B5), on a light, near-white background with a subtle gradient. Plenty of
whitespace, crisp lines, professional, enterprise software aesthetic.
No company logos, no trademarks, no real photographs, no human faces.
Only the stage labels as text; no paragraphs.
```

## Generate via Microsoft Foundry (Option B)

Backend: **MAI-Image-2.5** (Microsoft Foundry, preview), deployed in project
`project-ai-demos` on resource `project-ai-demos-resource`.

| Setting | Value |
|---|---|
| Resource (account) | `project-ai-demos-resource` |
| Project | `project-ai-demos` |
| Deployment name | `MAI-Image-2.5` |
| Endpoint | `https://project-ai-demos-resource.services.ai.azure.com` |
| API path | `/mai/v1/images/generations` |
| Model version | `2026-06-02` (GlobalStandard) |

### Prerequisites

- The `MAI-Image-2.5` model is deployed on the resource. To deploy via CLI:

  ```bash
  az cognitiveservices account deployment create \
    --name project-ai-demos-resource \
    --resource-group <RESOURCE_GROUP> \
    --deployment-name MAI-Image-2.5 \
    --model-name "MAI-Image-2.5" \
    --model-format Microsoft \
    --model-version 2026-06-02 \
    --sku-name GlobalStandard --sku-capacity 1
  ```

  Available regions (global standard): West Central US, East US, West US, West
  Europe, Sweden Central, South India, UAE North.
- `Cognitive Services Contributor` to deploy; `Cognitive Services User` (or an API
  key) to generate.
- Python with `requests` (and `azure-identity` if using Entra ID auth).

### Run

The helper script lives at [scripts/generate_banner.py](scripts/generate_banner.py).
It supports **API key** or **Microsoft Entra ID** (`DefaultAzureCredential`) auth.

```powershell
# Option A — Entra ID (recommended; run `az login` first, no secret needed)
python .github/skills/doc-banner/scripts/generate_banner.py `
  --prompt-file banner-prompt.txt `
  --out images/story-journey-banner.png `
  --width 1344 --height 768

# Option B — API key (set the key in the terminal; never commit it)
$env:AZURE_API_KEY = "<your-key>"
python .github/skills/doc-banner/scripts/generate_banner.py `
  --prompt-file banner-prompt.txt --out images/story-journey-banner.png
```

Defaults baked into the script: endpoint
`https://project-ai-demos-resource.services.ai.azure.com`, deployment
`MAI-Image-2.5`, size `1344×768`. Override via `--endpoint`, `--deployment`,
`--width`, `--height`, or the `AZURE_ENDPOINT` / `DEPLOYMENT_NAME` env vars.

## Output & embed conventions

- **Path:** save to `images/<doc-slug>-banner.png` for repo-root docs (e.g.
  `images/story-journey-banner.png`), or
  `agent-output/<engagement>/images/<doc-slug>-banner.png` for engagement docs.
- **Embed:** insert a markdown image **directly under the document H1**:

  ```markdown
  # The Bigger Story — Where This Template Fits

  ![Five-stage delivery flow: Base, Standardize, Plan, Land, Deploy](images/story-journey-banner.png)
  ```

- Always provide descriptive **alt text** (accessibility) and keep any existing
  ASCII/Mermaid "flow at a glance" block as the **text fallback** — the banner is
  decorative.

## Regenerating

The banner is not "final art" — it's reproducible. To refresh it: edit the prompt
(in the doc or `banner-prompt.txt`), rerun the script, overwrite the PNG. Commit
the new PNG and the prompt together so the result stays reproducible.

## Security

- **Never commit an API key.** Prefer Entra ID (`az login` + `DefaultAzureCredential`).
- If using a key, set it as an env var in the terminal only; rotate regularly.
- Review generated images before committing — reject any output that renders a
  real logo, trademark, or recognizable face.
